---
name: agent-authoring
description: Guides creation of AI agent definition files (.claude/agents/*.md) and their GitHub agent stubs (.github/agents/*.agent.md). Use when asked to create, review, or improve an agent definition. Do not use for skill files (use skill-authoring), general documentation, or application code.
---

## Agent Authoring

Operational runbook for creating agent definition files that are token-efficient, cache-friendly, and verifiable.

### Step 1: Create the Agent Definition

Create the agent definition file at `.claude/agents/<agent-name>.md`.
Read `references/agent-structure.md` for the required file format, frontmatter fields, and section templates.

> **Concert repo note:** If this agent is being created inside the `he3-org/concert` repository, add the managed-content header as the very first lines before the YAML frontmatter. For any other repository, omit this header.
>
> ```
> <!-- AUTO-GENERATED BY CONCERT — DO NOT EDIT.
>      This file is managed by Concert and will be overwritten on `concert update`.
>      Any manual changes will be lost. To customize behavior, see docs/concert/README.md -->
> ```

### Step 2: Create the GitHub Agent Stub

Create a lightweight stub at `.github/agents/<agent-name>.agent.md` that redirects to the full definition.
Read `references/agent-structure.md` — see the "GitHub Agent Stub" section for the exact format.

### Step 3: Engineer the Agent's Context

Apply context engineering principles to keep the agent's token footprint minimal while maximizing effectiveness.
Read `references/context-engineering.md` for the progressive disclosure model, compression patterns, and what to include vs omit.

### Step 4: Design Tool Usage and Error Recovery

Define what tools the agent needs and how it should handle failures.
Read `references/tools-and-error-recovery.md` for tool selection, error handling strategy, and retry patterns.

### Step 5: Add Verification Steps

Every agent must verify its own output. Add explicit verification steps to the execution flow.
Read `references/verification.md` for practical verification patterns for coding agents.

### Step 6: Validate the Agent

Before considering the agent complete:
1. Verify the definition file has valid YAML frontmatter with all required fields (`name`, `description`, `tools`, `model`, `interactive_only`).
2. Verify the GitHub stub exists at `.github/agents/<agent-name>.agent.md` with correct redirect path.
3. Verify the stub contains `name` and `description` in frontmatter but does NOT contain `tools:` or `model:` (stubs are metadata-only).
4. Dry-run the execution flow: walk through each step as if you were the agent. Flag any step where the next action is ambiguous.
5. Verify every file path referenced in the agent (skills, specs, state files) actually exists in the repository.
6. Check that the agent declares clear boundaries — what it does NOT do.
